LAW AND MOTION, 302, MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR #5963; DEPT. 302 (302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ALEXANDER CU AND JOSEPH CU'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, ARE SUSTAINED AS TO ALL THREE CAUSES OF ACTION WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AND THEIR MOTION TO STRIKE IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT. THE COMPLAINT FAILS TO ALLEGE SUFFICIENT ULTIMATE FACTS AS TO EITHER ALEXANDER CU OR JOSEPH CU SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CLAIM FOR NEGLIGENCE OR VIOLATIONS OF PENAL CODE SECTION 245.6 OR CIVIL CODE 52.1. PLAINTIFFS ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND, IF THEY ARE ABLE TO DO SO, TO ALLEGE ULTIMATE FACTS SHOWING THAT EACH OF THESE DEFENDANTS, AS DISTINCT FROM CONCLUSORY ALLEGATIONS LUMPING ALL DEFENDANTS TOGETHER, ACTED NEGLIGENTLY TOWARD THE DECEDENT (FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION), PARTICIPATED IN THE ILLEGAL HAZING OF THE DECEDENT (SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION), AND ACTED VIOLENTLY OR THREATENED TO ACT VIOLENTLY TOWARD THE DECEDENT TO INTERFERE WITH HIS CONSTITUTIONALLY PROTECTED RIGHTS (THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR #5963; DEPT. 302 (302/HK)

MINI MINUTES:

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-15-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. STEVEN SHERIF ABERN of Haapala, Thompson & Abern, LLP, Tel No. (510) 763-2324 appearing for Plaintiff;
Atty. CONOR M. KELLY of Walkup, Melodia, Kelly & Schoenberger, Tel No. (415) 981-7210 appearing for Defendants Alexander Cu and Joseph Cu.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling (with the sole modification of granting 20 days leave to amend): As to DEFENDANTS ALEXANDER CU AND JOSEPH CU'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, the demurrers of Alexander Cu and Joseph Cu are sustained as to all three causes of action with 20 days leave to amend and their motion to strike is off calendar as moot. The complaint fails to allege sufficient ultimate facts as to either Alexander Cu or Joseph Cu sufficient to state a claim for negligence or violations of Penal Code section 245.6 or Civil Code 52.1. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they are able to do so, to allege ultimate facts showing that each of these defendants, as distinct from conclusory allegations lumping all defendants together, acted negligently toward the decedent (first cause of action), participated in the illegal hazing of the decedent (second cause of action), and acted violently or threatened to act violently toward the decedent to interfere with his constitutionally protected rights (third cause of action). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/HK)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-15-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. STEVEN SHERIF ABERN of Haapala, Thompson & Abern, LLP, Tel No. (510) 763-2324 appearing for Plaintiff;
Atty. CONOR M. KELLY of Walkup, Melodia, Kelly & Schoenberger, Tel No. (415) 981-7210 appearing for Defendants Alexander Cu and Joseph Cu.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ALEXANDER CU AND JOSEPH CU'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is taken Off calendar as moot. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-22-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. CONOR KELLY of Walkup Melodia Kelly Schoenberger, Tel No. (415) 981-7210 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT LAMBDA PHI EPSILON NATIONAL FRATERNITY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. The complaint pleads sufficient facts to support each of the causes of action. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-22-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. CONOR KELLY of Walkup Melodia Kelly Schoenberger, Tel No. (415) 981-7210 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - DEFENDANT LAMBDA PHI EPSILON NATIONAL FRATERNITY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is denied. Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts to support the claim for punitive damages. Plaintiff can seek punitives on the survivor claims, causes of action 2 and 3. Nothing in Penal Code sec. 245.6 or the Bane Act precludes the recovery of punitive damages. Plaintiff can seek attorney's fees per Civil Code sec. 52.1(h). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)
